value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Foreign visitors in Japan can access emergency medical assistance services including medical referrals, hospital arrangements, emergency patient transports, and support with reporting theft or loss of travel documents.

Japan Customs allows duty-free import of personal effects for both non-Japanese and returning Japanese citizens, subject to restrictions on prohibited items and import duty guidelines including allowances for household goods and alcohol.
Foreigners staying in Japan can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures (immigration clearance, customs declaration) and tax-free purchases during their stay.

Foreign nationals who need to obtain a short-term visa to Japan and currently reside in Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, United Kingdom, or U.S.A. are eligible to apply for a single-entry tourist visa online.
Average price for a night in a hostel in Tokyo is $109
A 2-day 1-night trip to Tokyo Disney Resort for three people costs around $1,400 USD
Most museums, temples, castles and gardens charge an admission of at least a few hundred yen
Admission ticket price is $27
A full meal in Japan can be found for approximately $10 CAD, including tax, at any time of day, including fast food and teishoku set meals
A Pasmo passport card can be purchased from ticket offices or ticket vending machines at Narita Airport, Haneda Airport, Tokyo Metro Pass Offices and major metro stations in Tokyo and Yokohama

As of 2026, many stations are reducing staffed ticket counters, so having an IC card like Suica can save travelers significant time. Suica can be used either as a physical IC card or via Mobile Suica on smartphones. Thanks to Japan’s nationwide IC card interoperability system, a Suica purchased in Tokyo can be used to ride subways in Osaka or buses in Fukuoka without issues. Standard physical Suica cards (not Welcome Suica) can be refunded before leaving Japan at JR East ticket offices known as Midori no Madoguchi.
The Japan Rail Pass is valid on almost all trains operated on the nationwide JR network, including shinkansen, limited express, rapid, and local trains, but generally not valid on non-JR trains.
Western-style hotels can be found in all of Japan’s major cities, usually located close to train stations
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
The Japan Rail Pass is highly recommended for travelers using the Shinkansen and other trains, allowing access to Green Car reservations and specific seat selections to enhance scenic rail journeys.
Western-style hotels can be found in all of Japan’s major cities, usually located close to train stations
Most western hotels in Japan follow the same practices as hotels worldwide, except tipping is not required.
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
The journey time by highway bus from Maebashi Station and Takasaki Station in Gunma to Narita Airport is approximately 2.5 to 3 hours.
Japan is an island country in East Asia, located in the Pacific Ocean, divided into 9 regions: Hokkaido, Tohoku, Kanto, Chubu, Kansai (Kinki), Chugoku, Shikoku, Kyushu, and Okinawa.
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Bowing is an essential part of Japanese etiquette and culture, with a lighter bow common among those you have a connection with.
Tokyo
In Japan, one in three respondents considered the lunch price range of ¥900 to ¥1,199 as expensive; overall, half thought that ¥1,200 was expensive.

For overall travelers in Japan, using an eSIM is the best mix of affordability and convenience for mobile phones. Journey Japan eSIM offers seamless connectivity with fast speeds and affordable plans.
You can buy Japan eSIMs online before your trip from providers like Journey Japan eSIM, which offers affordable plans with fast and reliable service.
The Japan Rail Pass provides seven days of travel for 50,000 yen for a non-reserved seat for an adult.

IC travel cards can be used to make payments at many convenience stores, shops, and vending machines. Physical IC travel cards can be purchased at station ticket counters or automatic ticket vending machines at most railway stations. A deposit is required to buy an IC travel card.
Japan is a cash society, but credit cards and debit cards are accepted in most places in Japanese cities.

Booking a connectivity solution such as NINJA WiFi before arrival allows travelers to move directly from customs to transportation without delays and ensures dependable internet access throughout Japan.
Prepaid data SIM cards for Japan typically offer plans such as up to 10GB of high-speed internet
Most stores in Japan accept credit cards such as Visa, JCB, and Mastercard, as well as contactless payment apps like PayPay, LinePay, Rpay (Rakuten Pay), and MerPay.
Gifu Prefecture charges visitors JPY 200 per night for all categories of accommodation
Cash is still widely used in Japan, making up 82% of all payments in 2018, so travelers should be prepared to carry cash as an alternative to digital payments.
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen can be covered in just over two hours
The Shinkansen, or bullet train, debuted in 1964 and connects Tokyo and Osaka in about four hours, providing high speed and dependability.
Many ryokans provide geta, wooden sandals, to wear along with the yukata when going outside.

Situated between the prefecture’s dual peninsulas, the capital city of Aomori in northern Japan
Use an IC card like Suica or Pasmo for seamless payments on buses, subways, and trains in Japan.
Traditional ryokans (inns) in Japan offer travelers a unique and immersive cultural accommodation experience.

Japanese people speak softly or do not talk at all in public spaces such as mass transit, streets, stores, coffee shops, and restaurants, making public places in Japan noticeably quieter than in the U.S.

IC cards like Suica, Pasmo, and ICOCA are rechargeable smart cards that can be used on trains, subways, buses, vending machines, and convenience stores across most regions in Japan, including inter-region travel such as Tokyo to Kyoto.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
In Japan, many businesses are cash-only, so travelers should be prepared to carry cash.

Eating like a local in Tokyo involves being adventurous, stepping outside usual tourist zones, trying counter dining, following locals, and exploring small local restaurants even if there is no English menu or limited English spoken by staff.

Restaurants specializing in various Japanese foods are found all across Japan, including sushi-ya, soba-ya, udon-ya, ramen-ya, kare-ya, gyudon-ya, tempura-ya, family restaurants, Chinese, Italian, French, and Indian restaurants.

Japanese convenience stores offer a huge variety of fresh, high-quality food including onigiri, sushi, sandwiches, salads, bento boxes, noodle dishes, single-serving fruit, baked goods, beverages, candy, and snacks at budget-friendly prices.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, involving a traditional multi-course meal that requires the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
A balanced meal in Japan should satisfy the five basic tastes: sweetness, saltiness, sourness, bitterness, and umami.
Ryokan typically charge per guest rather than per room, which can catch out guests at some small, especially family-run, accommodations that only accept cash.

A lot of restaurants and eateries close for a few hours after lunch time for a break and dinner preparations usually around 2:00 p.m., and reopen around 6:00 p.m. Many restaurants tend to be open for an hour longer on weekends, while spots in office areas often have shorter hours.
